results.title: Data Scientist (Gen AI Governance) – Career Center | Mississippi State University
highlights:
Data Scientist (Gen AI Governance) – Career Center | Mississippi State University
...
# Data Scientist (Gen AI Governance)
...
At PNC, our people are our greatest differentiator and competitive advantage in the markets we serve. We are all united in delivering the best experience for our customers. We work together each day to foster an inclusive workplace culture where all of our employees feel respected, valued and have an opportunity to contribute to the company’s success. As a Data Scientist within PNC’s Data and Automation organization, you will be based in Pittsburgh, PA, Strongsville, OH, Birmingham, AL, Dallas, TX, or Denver, CO.
...
The Data Scientist plays a critical role in ensuring that Technology and Generative AI (GenAI) solutions comply with enterprise governance, risk, and control standards. This role is responsible for evaluating complex data sets, models, designs, and GenAI use cases to assess risk and control effectiveness, validate evidence, and provide actionable feedback to solution owners and governance stakeholders.
...
Develops algorithms using advanced mathematical and statistical techniques like machine learning to predict business outcomes and recommend optimal actions to management.
...
Qualifications & Experience:
...
Experience in AI/ML or GenAI governance, model risk management, or technology risk management.
...
Strong background in risk assessment, control evaluation, and issue remediation.
...
Familiarity with enterprise governance frameworks and audit concepts.
...
Exposure to AI/ML lifecycle tooling and model documentation is a plus
...
Performs analytical tasks on vast amounts of structured and unstructured data to extract actionable business insights.
...
Participates in the data gathering, data processing and data mining of large and complex datasets.
...
Develops algorithms using advanced mathematical and statistical techniques like machine learning to predict business outcomes and recommend optimal actions to management.
...
Runs analytical experiments in a methodical manner to find opportunities for product and process optimization. Assists in the presentation of business insights to management using visualization technologies and data storytelling.
...
May partner with Data Architects, Data Analysts, Data Engineers and Visualization Experts to develop data-driven solutions for the business.
...
Successful candidates must demonstrate appropriate knowledge, skills, and abilities for a role. Listed below are skills, competencies, work experience, education, and required certifications/licensures needed to be successful in this position.
...
Analytical Thinking, Competitive Advantages, Data Analytics, Data Governance Framework, Data Mining, Data Science, Generative AI, Machine Learning (ML), SAS Model Risk Management, Technology Risk Management
...
Data Architecture, Data Mining, Disruptive Innovation, Information Capture, Machine Learning, Modeling: Data, Process, Events, Objects, Prototyping, Query and Database Access Tools
...
Roles at this level typically require a university / college degree, with 3+ years of relevant / direct industry experience. Certifications are
...
desired. In lieu of a degree, a comparable combination of education, job specific certification(s), and experience (including military service) may be considered.
...
Base Salary: $86,250.00 – $158,125.00
...
, this opening is expected to be posted for two business days from 06/01/2026, although it may be longer with business discretion.

results.title: Data Architect-BigData, Hadoop, Python, Pyspark 4 Locations | Quickswoop
highlights: . As a Data Architect within PNCs Data Products Organization, you will be based in Dallas, TX, Pittsburgh, PA, Strongsville, OH or Birmingham, AL. Skills - Extensive experience with big data technologies and distributed computing tools (Hadoop, Spark, Hive Kafka, etc.) - Strong proficiency in relevant programming languages such as Python, PySpark, Spark, SQL - Proficient in Containerization (OpenShift, Kubernetes, Docker, etc.) - Experience with public cloud platforms – Apache Ice-burg and AWS - S3 - Knowledge of Unix/Linux shell scripting Act as an internal consultant for other data engineering and technical teams to enhance and optimize the quality of their code, ensuring adherence to engineering best practices. - Proven data literacy skills to describe business outcomes, data uses, data sources, and their management concepts and analytical approaches to all organizational levels in both business and technology. - Demonstrate initiative to develop approaches to solutions as part of a team; Review architecture and identify areas for automation, optimization, right-sizing, and Tasklance reduction to support health of the environment. - Design, build and test data integration processes between business-critical applications to solve complex problems within financial services. - Define and implement internal process improvements through the development of detailed analytics that provide actionable insights into key business performance metrics PNC is an in-office company that fosters a supportive culture where employees can thrive and achieve balance. We encourage candidates to connect with their recruiter and hiring manager to understand workplace expectations and ensure the role aligns with their goals. PNC will not provide sponsorship for employment visas or participate in STEM OPT for this position. Job Description • Designs, develops and optimizes data warehouses, with flexible and scalable data and ETL architecture, to support business users and Business Intelligence (BI) applications. • Analyzes business intelligence data and makes recommendations for data warehouse growth and integration an application/business function. • Works with IT and business customers to capture requirements for designing the data warehouse architecture. • Provides support and technical guidance to ensure the solutions are aligned with the architecture and framework. • Leads and consults on the implementation and integration of data warehouses; ensures the solution meets organizational quality and integrity standards. PNC Employees take pride in our reputation and to continue building upon that we expect our employees to be: • Customer Focused - Knowledgeable of the values and practices that align customer needs and satisfaction as primary considerations in all business decisions and able to leverage that information in creating customized customer solutions. • Managing Risk - Assessing and effectively managing all of the risks associated with their business objectives and activities to ensure they adhere to and support PNCs Enterprise Risk Management Framework. Qualifications Successful candidates must demonstrate appropriate knowledge, skills, and abilities for a role. Listed below are skills, competencies, work experience, education, and required certifications/licensures needed to be successful in this position. Preferred Skills Competitive Advantages, Customer Solutions, Design, Enterprise Architecture Framework, Machine Learning (ML), Risk Assessments, Technical Knowledge Competencies Consulting, Data Architecture, Database Structures, Data Warehousing, IT Architecture, Logical Data Modeling, Organizational Leadership, Problem Solving, Requirements Analysis Work Experience Roles at this level typically require a university / college degree, with 5+ years of industry-relevant experience. Specific certifications are often required. In lieu of a degree, a comparable combination of education, job specific certification(s), and

results.title: Hari Kiran Vaddi Gangaraju
highlights:
Ms · R
...
Data Scientist- AI ML Engineer at [PNC](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pnc-bank)
...
AI / ML Engineer with 10+ years architecting, building, and deploying production-grade ML, deep learning, and Generative AI systems across banking, healthcare, retail, insurance, and energy. Hands-on across the full lifecycle: data ingestion, feature engineering, experimentation, model training, deployment, monitoring, drift detection, and retraining at scale, partnering with data engineering and DevOps teams to ship models in highly regulated production environments. Deep banking and financial-services expertise across real-time fraud detection, anti-money laundering (AML), credit risk scoring, loan default forecasting, regulatory document intelligence, and customer behavior baseline modeling, with full SHAP / LIME explainability, fairness testing, and disparate impact analysis aligned to Fair Lending, ECOA, BSA, and internal AI governance standards. Strong polyglot engineer in Python, Java / Spring Boot, SQL, R, and PySpark, building API-first microservices and event-driven architectures on Docker, Kubernetes, and OpenShift. Hands-on with Generative AI and Agentic AI frameworks LLMs (GPT-4, Claude, LLaMA, BERT, RoBERTa), RAG, GraphRAG, hybrid search, LoRA / PEFT / QLoRA fine-tuning, prompt engineering, MCP, and multi-agent orchestration through LangChain and LangGraph. Production MLOps / LLMOps experience on AWS SageMaker, AWS Bedrock, GCP Vertex AI, and Azure AI, with MLflow, Kubeflow, Airflow, Feast feature stores, vLLM / TGI serving, Weights & Biases, DVC, and full CI/CD on Jenkins and GitHub Actions. Skilled in causal inference, A/B testing, Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), Monte Carlo Simulation, time series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM), and classical ML (XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, Logistic Regression) for pricing, promotions, demand, churn, and risk problems. Operates with an MVP-first, rapid-iteration mindset within agile cross-functional squads, partnering with data engineering, DevOps, risk, compliance, product, and business stakeholders. Strong record translating ambiguous business requirements into deployable AI products, simulating impact of decisions, and presenting insights to senior leadership through Tableau, Power BI, AWS QuickSight, and Plotly Dash.
...

PNC Financial Services (PNC) is a Financial Services company. PNC Bank offers a wide range of personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, mortgage loans, auto loans and much more. PNC Financial Services employs 34,174 people and has an annual revenue of $23.1B, founded in 1982. Headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States, operates in 46 countries (including Canada, India, United Kingdom, Colombia, and Cambodia). Has $6.9M in total funding, with 2 prior funding rounds.

results.title: Dhruv Raghav
highlights:
Data Scientist | ML & LLM Engineer | RAG, NLP, XGBoost | Credit Risk & Fraud Analytics | AWS
...
Data Scientist at [PNC](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pnc-bank)
...
Data Scientist with ~5 years of experience building and deploying scalable machine learning systems across banking, telecom, and retail domains. At PNC, I work on credit risk and fraud analytics, developing end-to-end ML solutions that directly influence business decisions. My work has improved loan decision accuracy by 27% and reduced fraud response time by 35% through real-time data pipelines and predictive modeling. I specialize in: • Machine Learning: XGBoost, LightGBM, Scikit-learn, feature engineering, model optimization • Generative AI: Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), NLP • Data Engineering: ETL pipelines, Apache Spark, real-time streaming systems • MLOps & Cloud: AWS (Lambda, S3, Kinesis, SageMaker), Docker, scalable deployment • Explainable AI: SHAP, LIME for interpretable and compliant ML systems My recent focus has been on building LLM-powered systems that enable natural language interaction with complex data, making analytics faster, more accessible, and more intuitive for decision-makers. I enjoy working at the intersection of machine learning, data engineering, and real-world business problems — transforming large-scale, messy data into reliable, production-ready systems. Open to opportunities in Data Science, Machine Learning, and AI/ML Engineering roles. 📩 Open to collaboration, opportunities, and conversations around ML, LLMs, and scalable AI systems.

I worked on real-world projects involving NLP, computer vision, and multimodal systems, using tools like Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Hugging Face. I built optimized translation, plagiarism detection, and emotion recognition models, and developed dashboards and predictive models for business and geospatial analytics. This program sharpened my skills in building scalable AI solutions and translating data into actionable insights.

I’m a Machine Learning Engineer with over 4 years of experience developing and deploying production-scale AI systems across NLP, anomaly detection, and predictive modeling. ### Machine Learning Engineer at [PNC](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pnc-bank) (Current)
...
Designed and optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines for querying unstructured financial documents (loan applications, regulatory filings, KYC/AML records), improving recall by 22% over a BM25 baseline in compliance pilot evaluations. Built an AWS-based document ingestion and indexing pipeline using S3, Textract, Lambda, and OpenSearch, enabling scalable hybrid (keyword + vector) retrieval across compliance and risk workflows. Implemented hybrid retrieval architecture combining BM25 and dense vectorsearch (cosine similarity via FAISS/OpenSearch), improving recall while maintaining exact-match regulatory traceability. Created document-aware chunking strategies (table-aware parsing, header preservation, cross-reference linking), increasing retrieval precision by 18% versus fixed-length chunking in structured evaluation benchmarks. Integrated cross-encoder re-ranking (BERT-class models) to refine top-k retrieval results, improving answer faithfulness and context precision based on RAGAS metrics and analyst review. Developed entity extraction workflows combining AWS Textract and fine-tuned NER models (Hugging Face Transformers) to extract names, dates, and monetary values, reducing manual compliance verification effort. Orchestrated multi-step LLM pipelines using LangChain, including query routing, context assembly within token limits, structured output validation, and audit logging (retrieved chunks, prompts, model/version metadata) to support governance requirements. Built evaluation-driven regression testing (200+ test cases) measuring recall, groundedness, and hallucination risk; integrated into CI/CD workflow to strengthen model validation prior to deployment.

ai-in-risk

for-financial-activity-74418744525624
...
I’ll be hosting a workshop at the upcoming AI in Risk Management for Financial Institutions Conference, centered on a question I don’t see asked often enough:
...
Where is the real value in AI and does it actually persist over time?
...
There is no shortage of use cases. But many struggle to move beyond pilot, and even fewer deliver sustained value once governance, cost, and operational complexity are fully considered.
...
In this session, I’ll walk through a practical framework for:
...
• Deciding where AI creates durable economic and risk value
...
• Identifying attributes of use cases that survive real-world constraints and regulatory scrutiny
...
• Integrating AI into existing risk and model governance infrastructures
...
• Addressing the people side including trust, adoption, and accountability
...
Not every AI use case should be pursued, and some should be actively avoided.
...
This is not about chasing innovation for its own sake. It is about making disciplined decisions on where AI belongs and where it does not.
...
If you are responsible for risk, compliance, or financial crime and are trying to move from experimentation to something that actually sticks, this session will be highly practical.
...
Register here: https://lnkd.in/gyP25TD4 Looking forward to connecting with others taking a more grounded approach. #AI #RiskManagement #ModelRisk #AML #Governance #FinancialServices
